title: Data Engineer Graduate (173615)
description: Requisition ID **173615** \- Posted **05/26/2026** \- **Information Technology** \- **Ireland** \- **Dublin** \- **Dublin** \- **Colgate\-Palmolive** \- **Travel \- up to 10% of time** \- **Hybrid** No Relocation Assistance Offered Job Number \#173615 \- Dublin, Dublin, Ireland **Who We Are** Colgate\-Palmolive Company is a global consumer products company operating in over 200 countries specializing in Oral Care, Personal Care, Home Care, Skin Care, and Pet Nutrition. Our products are trusted in more households than any other brand in the world, making us a household name! Join Colgate\-Palmolive, a caring, innovative growth company reimagining a healthier future for people, their pets, and our planet. Guided by our core values—Caring, Inclusive, and Courageous—we foster a culture that inspires our people to achieve common goals. Together, let's build a brighter, healthier future for all. **Graduate Data Engineer, Analytics Team** **2 year fixed term contract** We have an exciting opportunity for a Graduate Data Engineer within our Analytics Team at Colgate\-Palmolive. Reporting to a Senior Data Engineer. The role will focus on implementing and orchestrating scalable data pipelines which deliver reliable and consistent data to Data Analysts and Data Scientists for use in dashboards and models respectively, which will drive insights for growth for our business partners. This role will also work with cross\-functional teams to gather, wrangle, cleanse, structure, and build data products that support the Commercial Analytics needs (use cases) and the broader organization. Build and maintain the foundational data analytics infrastructure that support and enable Commercial Analytics use cases. **The ideal candidate is:** Self\-motivated and can work well both independently and within a team. A self\-starter, who is passionate about data engineering \& analytics technologies, solutions\-focused and will act as a business facing ambassador to drive adoption of our solutions. Has excellent communication skills, is eager to learn, and has a natural curiosity for how Commercial data can be structured and visualized to provide insights for growth. **What you’ll need:** * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or Information Technology * Strong communication skills * Comfortable to learn new tools and be an ambassador for these tools to business stakeholders, demonstrating the business value * Basic proficiency in core programming languages (such as Python, Java, C\+\+), and good understanding of software engineering principles. * Solid SQL knowledge and experience working with relational databases, query authoring and data logic. * Familiarity with data pipeline, modeling, and workflow management tools in cloud environments (such as GCP, AWS, etc) * Basic proficiency of data visualization techniques \& tools such as Looker Studio, Tableau, DOMO, Sigma etc. is desired. * Intermediate knowledge of Excel / Google Sheet (i.e., standard functions – aggregation, pivots, look ups, etc.) * Ability to collaborate using Google Suite of tools is an advantage * Willingness and ability to experiment and learn new tools and techniques, including leveraging AI for IT and problem solving. * Attention to detail, and penchant for accuracy * Strong time management, organization skills and self\-motivation * Comfortable navigating ambiguity \- in business requirements, technology options and direction. **Our Commitment to Inclusion** Our journey begins with our people—developing strong talent with diverse backgrounds and perspectives to best serve our consumers around the world and fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels a true sense of belonging. We are dedicated to ensuring that each individual can be their authentic self, is treated with respect, and is empowered by leadership to contribute meaningfully to our business. **Equal Opportunity Employer** Colgate is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, ethnicity, age, disability, marital status, veteran status (United States positions), or any other characteristic protected by law. Reasonable accommodation during the application process is available for persons with disabilities. Please complete this request form should you require accommodation. \#LI\-Hybrid

title: Data Governance Analyst
description: **Role Summary** **JOB DESCRIPTION** We are seeking a talented Data Governance Analyst. As a Data Governance Analyst within our business **,** you will be responsible for developing, maintaining, communicating, and promoting data management processes and controls. You assist with defining policies, notices, and standards, as well as promoting ongoing enhancements throughout the organisation. You will work closely with stakeholders in our Business, Compliance, Risk, and IT teams to enhance our retention and monitoring approach, ensuring best practice in data management. **Responsibilities** **RESPONSIBILITIES** **Data Governance Framework \& Standards** * Promote and embed data governance standards across Irish offices, business units and functions * Enhance and maintain the CFIL data governance framework, ensuring it is clearly communicated and understood by all staff * Develop, publish and maintain best practice guidance via internal communications, SharePoint, and formal guidance documents * Inform and advise stakeholders on data protection and privacy best practice. **Data Management \& Protection** * Identify unstructured data across the business and develop plans for appropriate management * Assist with Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs), including documentation, risk identification, and tracking of mitigating actions * Support the end‑to‑end coordination of Data Subject Access Requests (DSARs) and other Data Subject Requests (including rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, and objection) in line with GDPR and internal procedures. * Maintain and update Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) in collaboration with business units * Support oversight of personal data processing activities across business functions and third‑party arrangements **Monitoring, Risk \& Compliance** * Prepare monthly management information that includes issue tracking, actions and any additional data protection information required for reporting * Develop and monitor systems and controls to detect and prevent poor data behaviours * Conduct audits to support the monitoring and enforcement of good data practices * Perform regulatory gap analysis against GDPR, DPC, DPO and CBI guidance, and develop remediation plans as required * Support investigation and appropriate reporting of data breaches * Clearly articulate data issue mitigation processes and escalation pathways. **Training \& Awareness** * Prepare and support the delivery of data protection training and awareness initiatives, including induction training and periodic refreshers. * Act as a first point of contact for routine data protection queries from staff, escalating complex matters as required. * Promote staff awareness and support managers with practical data protection guidance. **Business Critical Services Support** * Contribute to the delivery of CFIL outsourcing and operational resilience programmes as part of the Business Critical Services team. **Qualifications** **Requirements \& Skills:** * 2\-3 years’ experience in a data control, data governance, or data protection role, ideally in a financial services environment * Strong understanding of data protection principles and regulatory requirements; relevant qualification in Data Management or Data Protection desirable * Solid ICT knowledge, including how systems generate, store, and control data, and how technology can be leveraged to manage data risk * Good understanding of industry\-standard data governance frameworks and standards * Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret multiple data sets, conduct gap analyses, diagnose issues, and deliver practical, risk\-based solutions * Experience managing and influencing change to drive continuous improvement in a data\-focused environment * Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence business functions * Self\-motivated, organised, and capable of planning work around key project milestones.

title: Data Quality Analyst
description: We are seeking a proactive and detail\-oriented Data Quality Analyst to support enterprise data quality, governance, and data reliability initiatives across multiple business domains. This role requires a strong analytical mindset, excellent SQL capability, and the confidence to work across complex datasets, ambiguous ownership environments, and cross\-functional teams to drive data quality outcomes. Background as a Business Analyst would be highly advantageous. Key Responsibilities * Monitor, analyse, and improve data quality across enterprise platforms and reporting environments * Investigate data anomalies, identify root causes, validate fixes, and support incident resolution activities * Partner with Data Stewards to define data quality rules, controls, and governance standards * Work closely with Data Engineers and business stakeholders to resolve data issues and align on ownership and business impact * Gather and document business and data requirements from stakeholders across multiple domains * Support data lifecycle management and contribute to data product thinking and governance practices * Challenge data assumptions and proactively identify opportunities to improve data integrity, consistency, and usability * Communicate clearly with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders on why data quality matters and how issues impact the business **Required Skills \& Experience** Strong understanding of: * Data quality concepts including accuracy, completeness, consistency, and timeliness * Data governance principles and data management best practices * Data lifecycle management and data product thinking * Advanced SQL skills (non\-negotiable), including: + Querying and analysing large datasets + Investigating anomalies and performing root cause analysis + Validating fixes and reconciling data issues * Experience with: + Data platforms and databases such as Databricks + Reporting and analytics tools such as Power BI + Working across complex enterprise data environments * Exposure to: + Data observability and monitoring tools such as Monte Carlo or similar platforms would be advantageous Soft Skills \& Attributes * Strong stakeholder engagement and relationship\-building skills * A proactive, visible, and collaborative approach with a strong can\-do attitude * Comfortable working independently across multiple teams and domains * Strong attention to detail with an analytical and problem\-solving mindset * Ability to influence and drive resolution across teams without direct authority * Confident communicator who can engage with both business and technical stakeholders effectively * Someone who embeds themselves within the business and builds trusted working relationships across the organisation Apply Now

title: Deal Desk Analyst
description: **Job Title: Deal Desk Analyst** **Location: Dublin (Hybrid – 2\-3 days per week onsite)** **Duration: 11 months contract initially with possible extension** **Max. Budget: €69\.9k per annum \+ paid holidays \+ pension** **What you’ll do:** * The primary focus of this position will be to help structure profitable and compliant transactions that address the needs of a diverse set of internal corporate customers, maximize client's revenue, and increase the effectiveness and productivity of the North America Digital Marketing Sales force. * This role will act in a fiduciary capacity in managing first line approvals for the deal approval process, including deal structuring and accelerating deal closures. * This will be accomplished by meeting with your sales teams to identify Customers' critical business issues and assist in structuring transaction that meet both client's requirements and the Customer's needs. * Manage the day to day deal approval process, meet with sales teams, communicate via documented business case with cross\-functional stakeholders to articulate deal value in a consistent manner. * Draft and certify customer contracts. * Perform to a specified set of metrics and service levels. * Responsible for adherence to and compliance with policy and procedures. * Interact with internal stakeholders, including but not limited to Legal, Sales Operations, Sales Finance, Revenue Assurance, Business Units, Order Management, Credit and other functions. * Regularly communicate with your sales teams to ensure consistent application of process and policy. * Ensure that transactions adhere to client's internal revenue recognition policies and operational guidelines. * Prepare in\-quarter deal status and communication to management. * Evaluate current processes and offer recommendations for areas of improvement. **What's needed to succeed:** * Bachelors in Finance or Accounting or equivalent experience. * Proven experience in FP and A, Sales Finance, Sales Operations or Deal Desk. * Experience drafting and analyzing binding sales agreements. * Ability to work independently in a fast\-paced environment. * Demonstrated capability to build strong working relationships with internal stakeholders. * Experience in structuring multi\-element contracts including subscriptions, licenses, services and support. * Proficient in software revenue recognition accounting, SOP 97\-2 and related. * Strong communication, organizational and problem\-solving skills. * Excellent oral and written communication skills.

description: We welcome candidates who are confident in treating both **private and medical card patients .** * **Areas of Practice at Square Dental:** * General Dentistry * clear aligners * Cosmetic \& Restorative Dentistry (veneers, whitening, composite bonding) * Oral Surgery * **Our clinic offers two modern,fully equipped treatment rooms, advanced technology, and excellent commuting options.** * **Competitive Earnings: A generous percentage of gross income, tailored to your skills and experience** * **Marketing Support: Dedicated campaigns to attract and retain patients** * **In\-House Specialist Referrals: Collaboration with orthodontists, implantologists, restorative dentists, and hygienists** * **Professional Support: Structured diary management and mentoring to maximise clinical time and career growth.** * **Our ideal candidate would have:** * Perform general dentistry procedures with a focus on delivering high\-quality patient care. * Work independently and collaboratively as part of a dynamic clinical team. * Flexible start date, with an immediate start being ideal. * Available to work 2\-5 days per week. * Saturday work day options available also. * A pleasant manner and a good way with people * Excellent abilities for patient treatment planning and excellent rebook rateWe are happy to support work permits, however you will need to have IDC registration. title: Design Engineer
description: We believe the food processing industry deserves infrastructure as serious as its problems. We're well\-funded, high\-output and selective. **About the Role** We're looking for a Founding Design Engineer, the person who will own both the look and feel and the frontend code of the most advanced decision\-support platform in the food industry. That means shaping how plant operators and commercial teams interact with AI\-powered forecasts, and then actually building those interactions yourself. You'll work directly with research and product to turn dense numerical outputs and complex model results into interfaces that are fast, clear and genuinely useful, then ship them securely to factories across the globe. **Responsibilities** * **Own Design and Build End\-to\-End:** Drive the full UX process, from research and rapid prototyping through to production, maintaining a highly interactive application used by food processors worldwide. * **Make Data Legible:** Turn complex forecasting outputs into intuitive visual modules, including time series charts, year\-over\-year volume comparisons, global supply heat maps and quality metric dashboards, without dumbing anything down. * **Build the AI Interface:** Design and engineer the frontend for our conversational AI layer, accounting for the particular constraints of non\-deterministic outputs and streaming responses via SSE\-based incremental delivery. * **Handle Security at the Client Layer:** Work directly with Supabase and implement Row Level Security (RLS) filtering as a first\-class concern in every frontend build. * **Measure and Improve:** Use PostHog to track engagement and feature adoption, and feed those signals into fast, evidence\-driven iteration cycles. **Qualifications** * Demonstrable experience shipping complex B2B SaaS products, with strong command of both Figma and a modern frontend stack including Next.js, React, TypeScript and TailwindCSS. * Comfort working with large, complex datasets and the visualization libraries needed to make dense mathematical models feel intuitive. * Hands\-on experience designing for LLM\-powered systems, including streaming interfaces and agentic workflows. * A bias toward action and a track record of thriving in fast\-moving, zero\-to\-one environments. **Culture** High standards, high trust. A small team that works hard and looks out for each other. If you want to build something that matters for an industry that's been underserved by software, this is the role. **Benefits** 35 days of paid leave annually, full travel expense coverage, automated pension contributions, enhanced parental leave and regular company off\-sites.

title: DevOps Engineer
description: Vectra® is the leader in AI\-driven threat detection and response for hybrid and multi\-cloud enterprises. The Vectra AI Platform delivers integrated signal across public cloud, SaaS, identity, and data center networks in a single platform. Powered by patented Attack Signal Intelligence, it empowers security teams to rapidly prioritize, investigate and respond to the most advanced cyber\-attacks. With 35 patents in AI\-driven threat detection and the most vendor references in MITRE D3FEND, organizations worldwide rely on the Vectra AI to move at the speed and scale of hybrid attackers. For more information, visit www.vectra.ai. Position Overview DevOps Engineers are critical when it comes to keeping both our customers and engineering team running. As part of the DevOps group you will work on products critical to our operations and software that proactively detects issues with systems in the data center, in the cloud, or at customer sites. **Responsibilities** DevOps Engineers will have a variety of responsibilities which may include things like: * Design and implement pipelines and tools to ensure continuous integration, delivery, and deployment for our products * Define and implement tools that enable other teams to follow DevOps best practices * Own and deliver a suite of platform services consumed by internal stakeholders across teams and functions * Work on cloud environments ensuring reliability and availability * Ensure production deployments are fast, reliable, and safe * Work with geo\-distributed development teams to improve quality and time\-to deploy * Automate, Automate, Automate **Qualifications** * Required + Relevant Bachelor's degree (Computer Science, Engineering, etc.) + 1\-2 years hands\-on DevOps/SRE experience + Belief in DevOps and Agile principles + Zealous about automation + Comfort in dealing with Cloud deployments, networking, and security best practices + CI/CD pipeline experience (GitHub Actions, Jenkins, etc.) + Hands on experience on Amazon Web Services (AWS) + Hands on experience with Kubernetes + Linux system proficiency * Preferred + Strong container/serverless experience + Proficient knowledge of Python language + Experience on Hashicorp ecosystem \- Terraform, Packer, Consul, Vault + Experience with telemetry tools/pipelines (metrics, logs, and traces) + Experience with RDS databases Vectra provides a comprehensive total rewards package that supports the financial, physical, mental and overall health of our employees and their families. Compensation includes competitive base pay, incentive plan eligibility, and participation in the employee equity plan (stock options). Specific benefits offered varies by location, but commonly include health care insurance, income protection / life insurance, access to retirement savings plans, behavioral \& emotional wellness services, generous time away from work, and a comprehensive employee recognition program. Vectra is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.

title: DevOps Engineer (Brightflag)
description: **Location \& Eligibility** This role offers a flexible work location across Ireland. Whether that be in the office, remote from anywhere in Ireland, or a mix of office/remote work in Ireland, you choose what works best for your lifestyle. Your time is valuable. To help with your application, we advise that regrettably we cannot offer work permit sponsorship/ self sponsorship for this role. **The Opportunity** Brightflag is growing. As a market leader, with a product that customers truly love, we’re scaling quickly. To continue to build and evolve our AI\-first product we’re growing across multiple disciplines. We have an exceptional team and a high bar. We challenge ourselves with ambitious goals, best practice standards and a drive for continuous improvement. If you want to grow, get better and accelerate your career in Engineering, Brightflag is the place for you. As a DevOps Engineer at Brightflag, you will play a vital role in shaping and maintaining our infrastructure and ensuring the smooth delivery of our cutting\-edge legal technology solutions. You will support the production infrastructure deployment, monitoring and troubleshooting for our AI\-powered platforms. **What You Will Be Doing** * Design, build, and maintain the infrastructure and systems necessary for the development, testing, and deployment of Brightflag's software applications. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams, including developers, QA engineers, and product managers, to define infrastructure requirements, implement solutions, and ensure smooth operations. * Develop and maintain CI/CD pipelines, automated deployment scripts, and configuration management systems to streamline software releases and updates. * Implement and maintain observability solutions to proactively identify and resolve system issues, bottlenecks, and performance optimizations. * Continuously improve the scalability, reliability, and security of our infrastructure by leveraging best practices and implementing appropriate tools and technologies. * Stay up to date with industry trends, tools, and practices related to DevOps and cloud infrastructure management, and apply that knowledge to improve our systems and processes. * Collaborate with the security team to ensure compliance with security standards and best practices, including vulnerability management and incident response. * Document system configurations, processes, and troubleshooting procedures to facilitate knowledge sharing and maintain a robust knowledge base. **Skills \& Experience** To be successful in the role, you need: * 3\-4 years experience as a DevOps Engineer * Competent programming skills of applicable languages (Java, C\#) and scripting ( python, sh/bash etc) * Good knowledge of linux operating system, e.g. common terminal commands * Solid knowledge of CI/CD principles and implementation ecosystems (AWS Codebuild, Bitbucket pipelines, Github Actions), including experience with build tools ( i.e. maven, make, npm, etc) * Understanding of fundamental cloud (AWS) infrastructure and principles, including clustering and scaling strategies. * Familiar with TCP/IP based networking and supporting internet systems ( DNS etc), and common protocols such as HTTP * Good knowledge of applicable web application technologies (application servers, databases, queues and caching) * Strong analytical problem solving skills. * Demonstrated experience in driving improvements to prevent issues before they occur * Self\-starter with experience taking ownership of projects through to delivery * Excellent communication and collaboration skills * Understanding of the software development lifecycle and company’s compliance processes (ISO27001 \& Soc2\) **Life @ Brightflag** We’re growing fast and so is the experience we can offer you: * A huge opportunity to make a real impact, to shape what we do and where we are going. * The exposure and challenge you need to learn, grow and progress your career in a high growth environment. * Complex technical and business problems to solve and the trust and autonomy you need to go and solve them. * A sound, helpful team, in a friendly, values\-driven and inclusive environment. * Competitive salary. * 25 days holidays. * Employer pension scheme. * Comprehensive health insurance, life insurance and long term illness/income protection. * Fully flexible work location and work patterns so you can balance life at home with life at work \- come to the office, work at home… or enjoy a blend of both at your discretion. * The Tax\-Saver or Cycle\-to\-Work Scheme. We are a diverse and inclusive bunch of people. We welcome diverse perspectives and people who make every day count and strive for constant improvement. We do not discriminate based on gender, ethnicity, sexual orientation, religion, civil or family status, age, disability, or race. **About Brightflag** Hi, we’re Brightflag, the AI\-powered, enterprise legal management platform that helps corporate legal departments operate with greater clarity, efficiency, and control. Powered by our patented AI, which was developed with over 100,000 hours of legal and engineering expertise, and supported by our best\-in\-class Customer Success teams, Brightflag enables in\-house legal teams to streamline operations, manage matters and spend, and collaborate more effectively with outside counsel. Today, Brightflag is trusted by leading global organizations and forms part of the Legal \& Regulatory division of Wolters Kluwer, a global provider of professional information, software solutions, and services. You can learn more about Brightflag here. **Our Interview Practices** --------------------------- *To maintain a fair and genuine hiring process, we kindly ask that all candidates participate in interviews without the assistance of AI tools or external prompts. Our interview process is designed to assess your individual skills, experiences, and communication style. We value authenticity and want to ensure we’re getting to know you—not a digital assistant. To help maintain this integrity, we ask to remove virtual backgrounds and include in\-person interviews in our hiring process. Please note that use of AI\-generated responses or third\-party support during interviews will be grounds for disqualification from the recruitment process.* *Applicants may be required to appear onsite at a Wolters Kluwer office as part of the recruitment process.*

title: Digital Marketing Content Executive
description: Spanish Point is one of Ireland’s leading indigenous tech companies with three core focus areas: * The global music industry, through our market leading Matching Engine product. * European SaaS providers through our innovative SmartAIM Library product and our associated SmartAIM Framework services. * Digital transformation services for medium to large commercial and public sector organisations in Ireland, through our partnership with Microsoft. We are going through a period of sustained growth and are looking to recruit an ambitious Digital Marketing Content Executive to help continue that journey. The Digital Marketing Content Executive will implement and showcase best in class audience led content, in order to attract, engage and convert key target audiences. The successful candidate will be responsible for creation, performance monitoring and optimisation of user centric content across Spanish Point’s multiple websites, platforms and social media channels in line with the organisation’s digital marketing strategy. **RESPONSIBILITIES:** * Develop and implement relevant, engaging and memorable content across digital channels, including case studies, videos, website copy and news articles. * Work collaboratively across teams and subject areas to develop comprehensive content and communications, drawing on technical expertise of software developers and product specialists. * Stay up to date with B2B marketing, social media and technology industry trends. Recommend new social media channels and campaign ideas to maintain a competitive edge. * Write, edit and proofread content across Spanish Point’s digital landscape (both web and social). Maintain responsibility for flawless copychecking processes in content publishing processes. * Create content with copy and creative that is tailored towards target audiences outlined in our audience segmentation strategy. * Working with the marketing team, champion a customer focused mindset, with win:win content creation based on insight and analytics. * Liaise with in\-house designer, external photography and video teams as required for asset creation. * Monitor performance, analyse results and make recommendations to optimise social media and website content. * Track and improve performance against set KPIs including social media channel growth, engagement rates and lead generation. * Monitor budget allocation across social media campaigns to achieve maximum impact of marketing spend. **SKILLS, KNOWLEDGE AND EXPERIENCE:** * You will have at least 3 years of experience in technology related product and services marketing (social media, digital and content marketing campaigns). * Proven expertise in turning complex information into engaging, digestible, audience\-led content. * Ability to work within an established tone of voice and apply brand guidelines consistently. * Strong understanding of social media including content development and social scheduling and measurement tools. * Proficiency in Google Analytics, Semrush other tracking tools. * You will have excellent English language presentation, written, and verbal communication skills, with an eye for quality and attention to detail. * Previous experience working within a Microsoft partner ecosystem, SaaS, or B2B Marketing. **NICE TO HAVE:** * Familiarity with website CMS including Sitecore or similar platforms. * Familiarity with email marketing including ClickDimensions or similar B2B automation and ecomms platforms. * Demonstrable experience collaborating with internal cross\-functional teams and external partners. * Bachelor's degree in marketing and/or Technology. **BENEFITS:** * Working arrangement: Hybrid – minimum of 3 days per week in office. * Perks include: Financial support for professional development, Annual Performance Reviews, 24/7 Employee Assistance, Health Insurance, Life Assurance, Cycle to Work Scheme, Sports \& Social Activities (Join our Green Team, Yoga or Hiking Club), Free Canteen, Referral Bonus Scheme **Application Process** We are accepting applications until August with a plan to complete the recruitment process by the end of that month. Please send applications and/or questions to recruitment@spanishpoint.ie with a subject containing “Digital Marketing Content Executive ”.

title: EV Operations Engineer
description: **The Role:** Join a fast\-growing tech company delivering EV charging solutions across Ireland. This role blends hardware, software, and customer support, giving you hands\-on exposure to connected devices and real\-world infrastructure. **Responsibilities:** * Provide **1st and 2nd line operations support** across EV charging hardware and software platforms * Troubleshoot issues related to **hardware, connectivity, networking, and user access** * Manage incoming support tickets via ticketing systems * Monitor live systems and proactively respond to alerts or faults * Support **remote diagnostics** , onboarding, and customer issue resolution * Coordinate with internal engineering teams and third\-party partners to escalate complex issues * Manage and support EV chargers installed at customer homes and commercial sites * Occasionally travel to customer sites to diagnose and resolve technical issues * Maintain accurate documentation and contribute to the internal knowledge base * Take full ownership and accountability for customer issues from start to resolution **Required:** * **Experience** in technical support, IT support, or field support roles * Degree in **Electrical Engineering, IT or a related field** * Strong troubleshooting skills across **hardware and software environments** * Experience using ticketing systems. * Excellent communication skills with a strong **customer\-first mindset** * Professional, outgoing personality with strong interpersonal skills * Knowledge in EV, IoT, telecoms, or energy sector is a plus *** Full, clean driver’s licence**

title: Engineering Graduate
description: Seeking an Engineering Graduate for a Dublin 24 role with a leading company offering excellent career development. * Salary: €34,000 * Location: Dublin 24 * Initial 9\-12 month graduate contract with potential for a permanent full\-time po **Celtic Careers** are currently recruiting for a **Engineering Graduate** on behalf of a leading manufacturer of renewable solutions for the commercial, residential, and industrial sectors. This is an office\-based role located in Dublin 24, offering graduates the opportunity to work alongside experienced HVAC professionals and develop their career within a well\-established and growing organisation. Key Responsibilities: * Support the sales network in the design and specification of HVAC systems * Assist with technical submissions and ensure compliance with project specifications and product requirements * Generate mechanical and electrical schematics * Support product installations to specification * Deliver product and project presentations to clients and consultants * Coordinate internally across departments to support complete project solutions Requirements: * Degree in Engineering with a focus on design, construction, renewable technologies, HVAC, or industrial engineering * Proficient in MS Office applications including Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and Outlook * Experience using CAD/AutoCAD * Strong communication and organisational skills This is an excellent opportunity for a recent engineering graduate looking to develop a career within the renewable and HVAC sector. For further information or to apply, please apply and submit your CV. *If you are living in Ireland and hold a VALID WORK PERMIT, we would love to hear from you, if however, you do not hold a valid work permit unfortunately we will not be in a position to assist you with your job search.* ***Celtic Careers is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer.*** *By submitting your CV or personal details, you are providing Celtic Careers consent to process your personal data. title: Engineering Resource Coordinator
description: **About the Job** The Engineering Resource Coordinator plays a key operational support role within the Engineering Studio, helping to coordinate employee, resource and development activities across a fast\-paced, project\-based environment. The role works closely with employees, studio leadership, HR and operational stakeholders to support the smooth coordination of people, onboarding, training and resource activities within a rapidly evolving business environment. This role is best suited to someone who enjoys working in a dynamic, collaborative and highly coordinated environment, is comfortable managing changing priorities, and values regular in\-person collaboration as part of day\-to\-day team operations. **About You** * Minimum 3 years’ experience in resource coordination, workforce planning, project coordination, operations support, or a similar role within a fast\-paced and dynamic environment. * Experience working within a matrix or project\-based organisation, where priorities, teams and business demands regularly shift. * Strong organisational and coordination skills, with the ability to manage multiple competing priorities and maintain structure in a fast\-moving environment. * Excellent verbal and written English communication skills, with the ability to build effective working relationships across multiple stakeholders, teams and levels of the organisation. * Comfortable working in a highly collaborative environment with regular in\-office interaction and cross\-functional coordination. * Proactive and solutions\-focused, with strong attention to detail and the ability to work independently within established processes and frameworks. * Comfortable managing a high level of coordination activity, employee interaction and changing operational requirements. * Experience supporting onboarding, training coordination, people operations or employee development activities would be an advantage. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office and resource planning systems preferred. * A degree in a related discipline is beneficial but not essential. Relevant experience and capability will also be considered. **Your Key Day\-to\-Day Activities** * Coordinate day\-to\-day resource and employee coordination activities across the Engineering Studio, helping to maintain visibility, organisation and consistency across teams. * Act as a key operational point of contact for employees within the Engineering Studio, supporting communication, coordination and employee queries. * Coordinate talent processes, including performance and reward cycles, ensuring activities are organised, tracked and completed in a timely and consistent manner. * Support onboarding activities for new employees in Ireland and internationally, helping to ensure a smooth and well\-coordinated employee experience. * Coordinate training and development activities for junior engineers, liaising with managers and stakeholders to support learning needs and scheduling requirements. * Maintain accurate employee, resource and coordination records across relevant systems and trackers. * Work closely with studio leadership, HR, Talent Acquisition and operational stakeholders to support changing business and resource requirements. * Support the implementation of people and resource initiatives across the Engineering Studio. * Assist in identifying coordination issues, scheduling conflicts or resource challenges and escalate where appropriate. * Contribute to continuous improvement of coordination, onboarding and people support processes. * Support a collaborative and engaged studio environment through regular in\-person interaction and day\-to\-day coordination with teams across the business. ##### **Why Join Us?** At Ethos, we’re dedicated to fostering an environment that encourages both **technical excellence and personal wellbeing**. **We offer:** * **Flexible Fridays** and a **9\-day fortnight working schedule** — enjoy every second Friday off to maintain work–life balance. * A culture that values **flexibility, learning, and teamwork**, with clear progression pathways and support toward **professional accreditation**. * Opportunities to work alongside some of the **most experienced engineers** in the industry, within a forward\-thinking, technology\-driven consultancy. **Benefits:** * Flexible working times * 20 Days holidays up to 25 days * Pension Scheme * Working travel out\-of\-pocket expenses * Travel tax scheme or bike\-to\-work scheme * Regular training/mentoring / further college support * Support for Chartership * Subsidised Health Insurance Allowance * A Professional Subscription Fee paid by the company * Paid Statutory Leaves * Healthcare check\-ups and flu vaccinations * Gym / Fitness membership * Heavily subsidised social committee (5 a side, golf society, climbing club and regular social events) Our vision is to: “**Be the company people want to work with, and for**” Our values differentiate and focus us every day to be EPIC \- for our people and our customers.

title: Facilities Services Helpdesk Officer
description: ### **Facilities Services Helpdesk Officer** **Role Objective:** To support The Bar of Ireland in delivering best\-in\-class estate services by coordinating an efficient helpdesk function, providing proactive and responsive facilities and building maintenance support, and ensuring accurate recording of facilities data. This role contributes to the effective provision of estate services for members, tenants, and staff through high standards of service delivery and operational excellence. **About the Role:** A dynamic and hands\-on role within the Estate Services team, reporting to the Estates Manager. This role offers the opportunity to work across a diverse estate portfolio, contribute to service innovation, project support and to play a key role in enhancing the experience of stakeholders. * *This role is titled as Estates Services Helpdesk Officer internally in our team* **Related Tasks Description \-** ***List is not exhaustive*** **Estate Helpdesk Operations** * Co\-ordinate day\-to\-day helpdesk operations, ensuring timely resolution of requests. Monitor service performance, analyse trends, and identify opportunities to improve service delivery. * Co\-ordinate PPM visits throughout each location to align with scheduled operations already planned within Estate operations activity. * Support the management of the access control system, BMS or other systems in operation, integrating data with the CAFM system as appropriate. * Analyse helpdesk activity, performance trends and user satisfaction levels. Engaging appropriate technology to review data and support planning; adopting automation and predictive analytics to improve workflow. **Estates Service Delivery** * Collaborate with the team on the delivery of FM operations to ensure all premises are run efficiently and costs are controlled. * Complete building inspection walks, including monitoring energy use and environmental reporting. * Support the running of functions and events through the preparation and organisation of rooms, function spaces, seating layouts library areas. Act as a key point of contact for CPD, Event and DDRC teams for sets up and logistics. * Maintain the building through the performance of light maintenance duties. * Assign tasks to our General Operative and prioritise delivery of actions as required to support to service delivery. * Assist in managing car park space and allocation and addressing any related issues. * Liaise on matters such as EV charging stations, allocation of spaces to staff and contractors and other car park–related services. * Liaise with Members/Members Relations on locker and seat allocation for designated properties. * Support project supervision as required to ensure timely completion of works. * Proactively advocate for compliance with EHS policy and procedures; escalating matters to our EHS Co\-ordinator as appropriate. **Administration and Reporting** * Support the department administrative needs of estate service, including PPM activity reports, and the issuance of Estates updates to stakeholders including tenants, members and staff. * Gather data for report preparation for Committee and Council meetings/updates as required. * Administer access controls systems and workspace facilities for members and staff. **T.E.A.M. Core Competencies** **Candidate Specification** *A suitable candidate for this role will have;* * Facilities Management qualification or certification or equivalent experience, * 2\-3 years relevant experience in similar role in a professional environment, * Experience in answering, evaluating, and prioritising incoming telephone, e\-mail, and in person requests for assistance from users experiencing facility management related queries, * Experience using FM Software products and the full Microsoft Office suite with particular emphasis on Excel, * Experience supporting or coordinating projects, * Excellent organisation and prioritisation skills and the ability to handle a busy, varied workload; Effectively prioritise workload to respond to daily demands, * Proven system administration and report writing experience, * Experience assessing, analysing and reporting on helpdesk activity and client satisfaction levels, * Experience using digital tools and technology to improve efficiency and reporting * Ability to manage relationships with a variety of stakeholders, * Excellent verbal and written communication skills. Open and engaged communicator with the ability to listen and respond with respect and clarity. Skilled at conveying information to members and colleagues, * Demonstrates initiative and works both independently and collaboratively, *Additional Experience that is desirable but not required;* * Trade experience or familiarity with DIY, carpentry or plumbing, * Experience using helpdesk software products/Snapfix software. **Role Type, Salary and Benefits** This is a full\-time permanent site\-based role. The successful candidate will work onsite in Church Street, Dublin 7, with working hours of Monday to Friday between the hours of 9am – 5\.30pm. The salary for this role will range between €35,000 and €38,000 depending on experience, with benefits including: * Excellent DC Pension Scheme * Additional holiday leave through Company days * Employee Assistance Programme * Death in Service Benefit * Income Protection Scheme * Digital Gym Wellbeing Studio * Professional Development and ongoing CPD opportunities * Bike to work and TaxSaver schemes * Service Recognition **Commitment to Equal Opportunities** The Bar of Ireland is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer that is committed to equality, diversity and inclusion in the workplace. The Bar of Ireland is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and neurodiverse conditions in our job application procedures. If you require assistance or an accommodation, you may contact us at HR@lawlibrary.ie. We are all too aware that imposter syndrome and the confidence gap can sometimes stop great candidates putting themselves forward for a position; so if this is a role that interests you, please do share your application \- we would love to discuss it further with you.

title: Facilities and Sustainability
description: **Facilities \& Sustainability Manager** ======================================== **Our people make the difference.** Here at PHX Ireland, we strive to be a place for everyone \- where each and every one of us can be our true, authentic selves, every single day. For us, individuality sparks new ideas, shapes new approaches and brings us powerful new perspectives. It's the heartbeat of our innovation, creativity, and success. Which is why, at PHX Ireland, we don't just embrace our differences \- we celebrate them. Across United Drug, McCabes Pharmacy, and TCP Homecare, in every location and every role, the way we work makes us who we are \- and that's something we are really proud of. Which is why we've summed up our ethos in our six PHX Ireland values; Customer focused, Quality driven, collaborative, ambitious, innovative, and inclusive. ***Bring your energy to our team, and make millions of lives better.*** **Why You'll Love Working With Us:** Family comes first: Life Stage Policies including maternity and paternity pay Perks you'll actually use: Generous in store discounts Pension Support: We contribute to your future We reward performance: annual bonus We've got your back: employee assistance programme and wellbeing initiatives **Belonging at Work**: Inclusive, welcoming team culture where you're valued for *you*. **What You'll Be Doing:** **Facilities Management** * Provide end\-to\-end facilities management oversight across a geographically dispersed, multi\-site portfolio including distribution centres, offices locations. * Own the operational performance of all hard and soft FM services, including building fabric, HVAC, electrical and mechanical systems, fire detection and suppression, access control, utilities, and grounds maintenance. * Develop, implement, and maintain planned preventative maintenance (PPM) and statutory inspection programmes to ensure asset reliability, safety, and compliance. * Lead and support the planning and delivery of CAPEX projects, including warehouse expansions, major refurbishments, plant and equipment upgrades, and critical infrastructure works, from business case development through procurement, delivery, commissioning, and handover. * Act as the accountable owner for facilities contracts, managing tendering, mobilisation, performance management, KPI/SLA governance, and continuous improvement of service providers. * Lead facilities budgeting, forecasting, and cost control, ensuring transparent cost models, robust financial governance, and delivery of value\-for\-money outcomes. * Identify and manage lifecycle replacement and capital investment programmes, including business case development, risk assessment, and post\-implementation review. * Ensure effective utilities management, including consumption monitoring, tariff review, anomaly investigation, and cost optimisation. * Maintain accurate asset registers, drawings, certifications, and compliance records, ensuring all facilities documentation is audit\-ready and aligned with internal procedures and Irish statutory requirements. * Support site openings, closures, refurbishments, and change initiatives, ensuring facilities readiness, compliance, and minimal operational disruption. * Collaborate closely with Operations, Security, Cleaning, IT, and H\&S teams to ensure facilities services support operational performance, resilience, and business continuity. **Sustainability \& ESG** * Develop, implement, and continuously refine the organisation's sustainability strategy, ensuring alignment with corporate ESG objectives, operational priorities, and regulatory expectations. * Lead energy, carbon, water, and waste reduction programmes across the estate, translating sustainability targets into practical, site\-level initiatives with measurable outcomes. * Own energy and environmental performance monitoring, including data collection, analysis, benchmarking, and reporting to support informed decision\-making and performance improvement. * Drive SEAI grant applications, audits, and compliance activities, ensuring projects are scoped, delivered, and evidenced in line with scheme requirements. * Support and deliver decarbonisation initiatives, including energy efficiency upgrades, electrification, renewable technologies, and low\-carbon infrastructure investments. * Work closely with Facilities, Operations, and Procurement teams to embed sustainability principles into capital projects, asset selection, and supplier engagement. * Track, analyse, and report sustainability KPIs, supporting internal management reporting, audits, and external ESG disclosures as required **Health, Safety \& Compliance** * Ensure full compliance with Health \& Safety legislation, fire safety, environmental regulations, and internal SOPs. * Act as a key stakeholder during audits, inspections, and regulatory engagements. * Manage risk assessments, incident follow\-ups, corrective actions, and continuous improvement initiatives. * Support emergency preparedness, business continuity, and site resilience planning. **What You'll Need to Succeed:** * Bachelor's degree in environmental science, Engineering, Facilities Management, or related field. * Proven experience in sustainability management and facilities operations, preferably in a warehouse or distribution environment. * Strong understanding of environmental regulations and compliance requirements. * Excellent project management skills, with the ability to prioritise tasks and meet deadlines. * Effective communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate across departments and influence stakeholders. * Analytical mindset with the ability to collect, analyse, and interpret data to drive decision\-making. * Demonstrated commitment to sustainability and a passion for driving positive environmental change. **Personal Attributes** * Highly motivated, self\-driven professional with a strong sense of ownership and accountability for outcomes. * A people\-focused individual who builds strong, trusted relationships across all levels of the organisation and with external partners. * An effective communicator and influencer, able to engage stakeholders, bring people on the journey, and drive behavioural change. * Proactive and solutions\-focused, with the ability to anticipate issues and take decisive action. * Highly organised with strong attention to detail, capable of managing multiple priorities in a fast\-paced, regulated environment. * Comfortable operating at both strategic and operational levels, translating strategy into practical delivery. * Committed to continuous improvement, collaboration, and developing high\-performing, engaged teams. **About United Drug** United Drug is Ireland's largest pharmaceutical distribution company and was originally founded by a group of pharmacists in Ballina, Mayo in 1948\. In 2022 United Drug became part of the PHOENIX group and is now part of Europe's leading healthcare provider in the sector. Employing more than 650 staff across operations in Dublin, Limerick and Mayo, we partner with more than 1,900 pharmacies, hospitals, clinics and primary care centres across Ireland. Our business spans across three business units; United Drug Wholesale, United Drug Distribution and United Drug Consumer \- all leaders in their respective categories. **About PHX Ireland \& PHOENIX Group** We are comprised of United Drug, Ireland's leading pharmaceutical wholesaler and distributor; McCabes Pharmacy, Ireland's leading community pharmacy chain; and TCP Homecare, innovators in clinical home nursing services and direct to patient delivery solutions. PHX Ireland Group employs over 1,800 people, operates 5 distribution centres and pharmacies nationwide, delivering over 85 million packs to Irish pharmacies and hospitals and serving 500,000 patients each year. PHX Ireland is part of the PHOENIX group, Europe's leading healthcare provider. Headquartered in Mannheim, Germany, the PHOENIX group brings together more than 48,000 employees from 29 countries, includes 223 pharmaceutical distribution sites, and more than 3200 owned pharmacies and 17000 partner pharmacies. ***We strive to foster an open and inclusive environment, embracing applications from individuals of all backgrounds. Our commitment lies in ensuring an accessible interview process for all candidates. If you require additional support or accommodations to attend an interview, we kindly encourage you to reach out to*** ***jobs@phxireland.ie***

title: Finance Specialist - 14-month FTC
description: **Location: Dublin, London, Leeds** **Contract type: 14 month FTC** **Role Purpose** * This is a high\-impact operational role supporting Project Polaris, a flagship global finance transformation programme, gaining exposure to a greenfield SSC build and Oracle Fusion programme\-scale delivery. The role will generate lasting career capital in GBS and Transformation programmes. * Project Polaris is a critical strategic programme for Flutter Finance, this role has an integral part to play as part of the transition and creation of a flagship Global Hub. * This role will support finance process transition across the different towers in the Global Hub (incl. RTR, PTP, RTC). The role will be delegated into a specific tower/process. * The Sender holds responsibility for documenting processes by engaging with current team members as part of the knowledge transfer (KT) process, then preparing desktop procedures (DTPs) in collaboration with Receivers. * Raising issues to Sending Team Leads and supporting the transition of processes and knowledge to the receiving hub. * Demonstrate strong accounting expertise, process understanding, and emotional intelligence to facilitate effective knowledge transfer and collaboration. * Expect global programme exposure, ERP implementation experience, cross\-functional stakeholder management at scale, and involvement in a landmark GBS build. * Travel will be expected as part of this role given the global nature of Project Polaris. Travel may be required to US, UK, European and APAC locations. **Accountabilities \& Responsibilities** **Knowledge Transfer Delivery** * Collaborate with team members to understand processes and synthesise information into complete end\-to\-end process documentation and key information * Provide Receivers with complete and accurate information on processes, systems, and procedures conducted by Senders. * Responsible for structured and recurring onboarding of new hires across multiple waves * Review detailed Desktop Procedures (DTPs) in collaboration with Receivers to ensure completeness of information * Deep process To\-Be understanding, enabling the ability to identify redundant tasks to be stopped rather than transitioned * Attends and agrees completion of KT sessions * Updates KT tracker and escalates to team lead with urgency when required * Work collaboratively with Receivers to facilitate effective knowledge transfer and process transition. * Work together with Receivers to finalise list of internal and external stakeholders and processes prepared by Receivers. * Ensure accuracy and completeness of knowledge transfer deliverables; validate documentation quality. * Support the development of process documentation and training materials to facilitate knowledge transfer. * Coordinate with other Senders to ensure consistency and alignment in knowledge transfer activities. **Issue Escalation \& Progress Tracking** * Raise issues to Sending Team Leads when roadblocks or challenges arise during knowledge transfer. * Keep Sending Team Leads updated on progress on a daily basis, raising any issues or concerns. * Attend weekly calls with Sending Team Leads to provide progress updates and escalate blockers. **Skills \& Capabilities** **Essential** * Strong accounting process expertise and domain knowledge across either RTR, RTC, PTP, Tax, Treasury Internal Controls or Finance Tech; ability to develop deep understanding of assigned processes and procedures. * Experience creating and maintaining programme documentation, (e.g. project plans, risk registers, status updates) * Proven capability to work across cultures and geographies * Excellent written and verbal communication skills; ability to convey complex accounting and process information clearly and concisely. * Ability to work collaboratively with Senders and other Receivers; strong teamwork and partnership mindset. * Strong attention to detail and drive to improve quality; commitment to accuracy in knowledge transfer. * Process understanding and documentation skills; ability to articulate and document complex processes clearly. * Ability to raise issues and concerns clearly and professionally; proactive communication style. **Desirable** * Strong emotional intelligence; ability to build relationships, and manage interpersonal dynamics effectively. * Proactive learning mindset; ability to absorb complex information and develop capability quickly. * Resilience in fast\-paced environments; flexibility and adaptability to changing requirements. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Qualified or experienced accountant in finance operations or similar accounting role; with \~5 to 7 years PQE * Strong accounting knowledge and expertise in assigned process areas is essential. * Demonstrated experience in process documentation and knowledge transfer activities is essential. * Experience in a GBS, or large scale finance transformation environment is advantageous * Proven ability to work collaboratively across teams and locations; strong interpersonal skills. * Proficiency in ERP systems (Oracle experience advantageous); Strong Microsoft Office Skills * Exposure to the online gaming industry is an advantage * Ability to work effectively in a multi\-entity, multi\-location environment. * Strong communication and mentoring skills; ability to support learning and development. *If you are an internal candidate, please note that this opportunity will be processed in accordance with our internal secondment process. Further details will be provided by Talent Acquisition upon application.*

title: Finance Supervisor
description: **Finance Supervisor – Ballsbridge, Dublin 4** * **Location:** Ballsbridge, Dublin 4 (Excellent public transport links) * **Salary:** €45,000 \- €50,000 (DOE) * **Package:** Competitive Benefits \+ Rapid career growth potential * **Reporting to:** Financial Controller **About the Opportunity:** Looking for a career accelerator in hospitality finance? This is an exciting opportunity for an ambitious finance professional to join the team at the heart of Ballsbridge as a **Finance Supervisor** . This is the perfect stepping\-stone for a Senior Accounts Assistant or Assistant Accountant looking to take the next step into leadership. You will enjoy a highly visible role with a fast\-tracked route into a **Finance Manager** position, gaining exposure to high\-level month\-end and forecasting from day one. **The Role:** * **Team Leadership:** Supervise, mentor, and develop a small on\-site team (directly managing the Accounts Assistant and Graduate). * **Financial Ownership:** Take day\-to\-day responsibility for managing Accounts Receivable (AR), Accounts Payable (AP), and Income Audit processes. * **Month\-End \& Strategy:** Support the Financial Controller with Month\-End closure, forecasting, and driving financial systems efficiency. **What We Are Looking For:** * **Skills:** Strong working knowledge of day\-to\-day finance operations (AP/AR/Income Audit). Intermediate Excel skills and a tech\-savvy approach to finance systems are preferred. * **Mindset:** An organised, proactive individual ready to roll up their sleeves, take ownership, and lead junior team members.

title: Financial Advisor
description: We are currently working with a Wealth Management firm who are looking to hire a Financial Advisor to join their team in Dublin. This position involves managing an established client portfolio while also focusing on growing new business opportunities. Responsibilities of this **Financial Advisor** Job: * Manage and grow an existing portfolio of clients, ensuring a proactive client focused service. * Drive new business growth through referrals, self\-generated leads, and existing client relationships. * Ensure all client records are maintained accurately. * Conduct thorough research and analysis to recommend financial solutions that address a wide range of client needs. * Stay ahead of industry trends, regulatory changes and market developments Requirements of this **Financial Advisor** Job: * Fully QFA qualified * In\-depth knowledge of financial regulations, markets and industry practices * Demonstrable experience in financial advisory, with a proven track record of managing and growing client portfolios * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * A high level of commercial awareness For more information on this **Financial Advisor** Job or to discuss in more detail, contact Aoife Stokes at astokes@elevatepartners.ie or apply directly below.

title: Financial Analyst Group Reporting - Dublin
description: **Country:** Ireland **Req ID:** 524697 **Job Type**: Full Time **Workplace Type**: Hybrid **Seniority Level**: Entry Level **About CRH** CRH is the leading provider of building materials critical to modernizing infrastructure. with out team of 83,000 people across 4,000 locations, our unmatched scale, connected portfolio, and deep local relationships make us the partner of choice for transportation, water, and reindustrialization projects, shaping communities for a better tomorrow. CRH (NYSE: CRH) is a member of the S\&P 500 Index. For more information, visit www.crh.com. Without you noticing our products, we are everywhere you live, work, and relax. Our project portfolio includes some of the most sustainable and cutting\-edge building projects around the world. Think of the asphalt on the Silverstone Grand Prix Circuit, the Paris Metro Rail project, but also the Louis Vuitton Museum in Paris, parts of the Burj Khalifa, and the Kennedy Space Centre. The R\&PA team forms part of the CRH Group Finance function, is a high performing team and a valued business partner that leads a number of key internal and external reporting processes. This includes monthly Board and management reporting, financial planning and analysis, forecast and annual Budget; the team also prepares quarterly briefing materials to support external results announcements and business performance related sections of the 10\-Q/10\-K. The wider R\&PA team is divided up into two sub teams, Reporting and FP\&A, each of which has ownership for specific processes and deliverables and is the central liaison for the Divisions. **This role is a financial analyst position on the R\&PA Reporting sub\-team.** The Reporting team is responsible for: * Monthly **Board and management reporting** including review and analysis of their key performance metrics, providing detailed analysis and commentary to Board and senior management of drivers of performance; * Annual **Budget reporting** process including review and analysis of their key performance metrics, providing the related output reporting and analysis for Group preliminary and final budget to the Board and executive team; * Governance and reporting of **Capital Expenditure** for the Divisions, including tranche allocation and detailed analysis of major capital projects on a quarterly basis; * Building a collaborative and functional relationship with CRH colleagues in the Divisional Finance teams, identifying solutions and resolving complex issues as they arise; * Review of management to financial results reconciliation for the Division at quarter\-ends; * Provision of high\-quality and timely ad\-hoc analysis to senior management and business partners. Hybrid working at present, with the team ordinarily based at Group HQ in Stonemasons Way, Dublin. **Key Tasks and Responsibilities:** =================================== * Provide critical analysis for the monthly Board and management reporting processes including review of the results submitted by the Divisions and ownership of the related outputs with a key focus on critical trend analysis, key performance metrics and drivers of performance. * Ownership and review of the preliminary and final budget outputs for the Group including Board and management presentations, reports and analysis. * Review and analysis of financial reporting notes and disclosures, as part of the Group financial consolidation process. * Ability to identify and resolve reporting issues that arise, identifying solutions while working closely with Divisional Finance and other teams across Group Finance. * Assistance in system developments that are required in Hyperion Financial Management (HFM) for reporting purposes, liaising with the Hyperion support team. * Support the implementation of EPM for Reporting, actively involve in UAT, parallel run activities and Go‑Live readiness review, while help establishing aligned Management Reporting Guidelines to support a consistent, future‑state reporting framework. * Focus on continuous improvements in all processes and outputs to add value and drive business results. * Build strong relationships while working extensively with Divisional Finance teams on new reporting requirements and analysis in order to provide greater insight into their performance and results. * Provide high quality ad hoc analysis to senior management as requested to support decision making. **Key Competencies:** ===================== * Team player with ability to contribute both individually and as part of a team. * Demonstrate integrity and trust, with ability to maintain discretion and confidentiality. * Self\-motivated with a high degree of initiative; comfortable working in a demanding fast\-paced environment. * Strong communication skills and comfortable liaising with senior management. * Good financial and analytical skills, with excellent attention to detail and a focus on results. * Ability to multitask within tight deadlines. **Education and Experience:** ============================= * Qualified accountant with up to 2 years PQE * Strong academic background, professional record and qualification (ACA, CFA or equivalent) * Experience working on high performing teams, ideally across different geographies * Good systems capabilities and/or interest in financial reporting systems; experience with HFM system would be an advantage * Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint and Word * Exposure to Group reporting and processes. The nature of the role is outlined above. Flexibility is required within the role, which includes some core elements but also periodic emphasis on more project\-type activities. **What CRH Offers You** * A culture that values opportunity for growth, development, and internal promotion * Highly competitive salary package * Comprehensive secondary benefits * Significant contribution to your pension plan * Health and wellness programs, including an on\-site gym and fitness classes * Excellent opportunities to develop and progress with a global organization **Connect your future to CRH** We are curious to learn more about you. At CRH, we believe our mutual differences contribute to the healthy, productive, and enjoyable workspace we create. Please introduce yourself and send us your application. Following a positive review of your application, you will be invited to an introductory call with one of our Recruiters. Is this role not for you, but do you know someone who would love to join the team? Please let us know! CRH finds it important that vacancies are shared to individuals that may find them interesting and/or could be suitable for the role Please contact our recruitment team at careers@crh.com. *CRH is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to creating an inclusive work environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community.* *Benefits/perks listed above may vary depending on the nature of the employment with CRH and the country where you work.* ***Please note that we cannot accept any applications submitted through email for GDPR purposes. Candidates must apply through our job portal.*** ***We do not accept candidate introductions for this position from recruitment agencies, unless you have been instructed to do so by our recruitment team.***
